descriptors.text: I often notice physical sensations in my body — like hunger, heartbeat, or muscle tension — before I notice what emotion I am feeling
When plans change unexpectedly, even small changes, it takes me noticeably longer to adjust than it seems to take others
I often find myself understanding things through images, metaphors, or analogies before I can explain them in words
When something genuinely interests me, I can stay focused on it for hours without noticing time passing — but switching to something else can feel jarring
I sometimes struggle to put a name to what I am feeling — I know something is going on emotionally, but I cannot easily identify what it is
In social situations, I often put significant effort into acting in ways that feel expected or normal — and this can be exhausting even if the interaction itself was fine
My sense of time passing is unreliable — I frequently underestimate how long something will take, or lose track of time entirely when absorbed in something
I tend to notice patterns and connections between things — sometimes this is useful, sometimes I am not sure if the pattern is really there
Situations I see as unfair or ethically wrong tend to affect me more strongly than they seem to affect other people around me
I need regular exposure to new ideas, places, or experiences — routine without novelty starts to feel draining or suffocating
I spend a lot of time thinking about big questions — the meaning of things, why I am here, what happens after death — more than most people seem to
I use repetitive movements, sounds, or sensations to self-regulate — to calm down, focus, or process strong feelings — and this helps even if others find it unusual
Bright or busy spaces can feel manageable when I pace how much I take in at once
Layered sounds and lights sometimes blend together until I need a quieter patch to reset
I sense where my limbs are in space even with my eyes closed when I slow down and check in
After sitting still for a long stretch, standing up feels like re-learning where my weight sits
After lively gatherings I need quiet time before I feel ready for another round of conversation
Short check-ins with people I trust restore me faster than long surface-level chats
I appreciate when people say plainly what they need rather than hinting and expecting me to guess
Clear timelines and next steps help me stay aligned more than vague reassurances
One memory often pulls up another from a different season of life while I am doing something routine
I enjoy finding what two hobbies or interests share under the surface
Small wins can lift my whole day; small setbacks can sit heavy until I process them
I feel moved by stories or music in a full-body way that surprises people who know me casually
Repetition without variation makes my attention drift even when I care about the outcome
I add small twists to familiar tasks so they stay engaging enough to finish
I stick with hard practice when the activity itself feels meaningful, not only when there is an external reward
Curiosity alone has led me deep into topics no assignment asked for
Certain fabrics or temperatures on my skin distract me until I adjust clothing or the room
Hugs or handshakes land differently depending on my energy that day
Steady beats while walking or working help me settle into a workable pace
Irregular interruptions make it harder for me to find my stride again
I scan a scene for small changes before I relax into it
Typos or visual clutter pull my eye even when I am trying to focus on the main message
Imagining how a choice might feel next month shapes what I say yes to today
Concrete examples of outcomes matter more to me than abstract slogans
Seeing someone treated unfairly can stay on my mind after the moment has passed
I notice when rules apply to one group differently than another in everyday situations
Familiar rituals comfort me as long as there is still room for small surprises now and then
Too many new things at once feels like overload even when each part is interesting alone
I do my deepest thinking when I can control the pace and pause without explaining aloud
Background voices at low volume sometimes help me focus; sudden loud bursts derail me
Calm people nearby help my body settle faster than when I am the only regulated person in the room
I pick up tension in a group before anyone names it
I remember events as little stories with a beginning and turning point more than as raw facts
Telling someone what happened helps me sort what it meant for me
Certain textures, scents, or movements feel grounding when stress runs high
I seek gentle stimulation when the day has felt flat rather than overstimulating
Switching tasks costs me a visible beat; stacking too many switches in an hour wears me down
Finishing one block fully before opening the next feels kinder to my attention
I track give-and-take in relationships and notice when the balance drifts for a long stretch
Praise feels hollow to me when it does not match what actually happened
Some rooms feel like they fit my body and some feel like I am performing inside them
Changing seats or lighting can shift my whole capacity to participate
Upcoming events show up in my sleep or appetite before the calendar date feels close
Deadlines create a physical buzz that can help or overwhelm depending on what else is on my plate
I value feedback that names one concrete behaviour instead of a vague character label
I offer feedback in small doses because big speeches feel risky to receive and to give
I notice when I need a break from input before I feel completely depleted
Doing something with my hands helps me think through decisions that feel stuck in my head
